read_file.name: read_file
read_file.description: READ_FILE IS THE ONLY WAY TO READ FILES - DO NOT USE DIRECT FILE ACCESS
read_file.usage_example: Read index.js to see it has 156 lines and any surgical warnings

write_file.name: write_file
write_file.description: WRITE_FILE IS THE ONLY WAY TO WRITE FILES - DO NOT USE DIRECT FILE EDITING
write_file.usage_example: Write to index.js after obey_surgical_plan approval

obey_surgical_plan.name: obey_surgical_plan
obey_surgical_plan.description: CRITICAL: MUST call this BEFORE using write_file to validate your surgical plan
obey_surgical_plan.usage_example: Call with current_line_count=650 and estimated_addition=100 to check if adding 100 lines to a 650-line file is safe

preflight_change.name: preflight_change
preflight_change.description: CRITICAL: MUST call this BEFORE write_file for non-trivial changes
preflight_change.usage_example: Validate a refactoring before applying changes

get_file_context.name: get_file_context
get_file_context.description: MUST use this tool BEFORE refactoring - this is the ONLY way to get comprehensive file context
get_file_context.usage_example: Get context for a file before refactoring to understand what depends on it

analyze_change_impact.name: analyze_change_impact
analyze_change_impact.description: Analyze the impact of proposed changes on the codebase
analyze_change_impact.usage_example: Analyze impact before refactoring to see what depends on it

list_directory.name: list_directory
list_directory.description: List files and directories in a given path
list_directory.usage_example: Explore project structure before making changes

search_code_files.name: search_code_files
search_code_files.description: Search code files for a pattern with language-aware ranking
search_code_files.usage_example: Search for 'function' to find function definitions across all supported languages

refactor_move_block.name: refactor_move_block
refactor_move_block.description: Move a code block from one file to another while maintaining surgical compliance
refactor_move_block.usage_example: Move a 200-line function from index.js to utils.js to keep index.js under the limit

extract_to_new_file.name: extract_to_new_file
extract_to_new_file.description: Extract a code block to a new file while maintaining surgical compliance
extract_to_new_file.usage_example: Extract a large class to a new file to reduce source file size

enforce_surgical_rules.name: enforce_surgical_rules
enforce_surgical_rules.description: MUST call this tool BEFORE write_file - this is the ONLY way to validate your code against surgical rules
enforce_surgical_rules.usage_example: Validate code before writing to check for forbidden patterns

file_operations.description: Tools for reading and writing files

governance.description: Tools for enforcing architectural rules and validation

analysis.description: Tools for analyzing code structure and dependencies

search.description: Tools for searching code and patterns

refactoring.description: Tools for refactoring and code organization

heuristics.primary: If a tool exists for the task, use it
heuristics.secondary: If multiple tools exist, choose the highest-priority one
heuristics.tertiary: If the tool fails, retry with context
heuristics.fallback: If it fails again, use fallback tool
heuristics.never: Never hallucinate a tool or manually edit when a tool exists

conflicts.reason: Write_file enforces surgical rules and creates backups
Read_file enforces .sweignore rules and injects architectural context
